lesson: Sandbox untrusted generated code on Windows: launch CREATE_SUSPENDED, assign to a Job Object with a memory limit, then resume — never let it run unconfined.
Treat any non-zero exit code from a sandboxed untrusted child as a crash, not just timeouts, since it can't be trusted to signal failure any other way.
When reading length-prefixed binary results from an untrusted subprocess, bounds-check the declared count before allocating to avoid a crash on corrupt or truncated output.
Delete stale build artifacts before invoking a compiler so a post-run existence check genuinely proves this run produced them, not a leftover prior build.
cmd /c strips the leading and trailing quote from a quoted argument; double-wrap quoted compiler paths as cmd /c ""path\exe" args" to avoid mangled redirection.
Force a decimal point on codegen'd C++ float literals (e.g. std::showpoint) — a bare digit-sequence plus an f suffix like 2f is not a valid floating literal and fails to compile.
When crossing over two tree genomes of different arity, only graft donor subtrees whose variable references are valid in the receiver, or the offspring reads out of bounds.
Use bounded retry with a guaranteed fallback for mutation/crossover operators: attempt N candidates, validate each, and always return an unchanged deep copy rather than risk an invalid result.
